audit_items.proof_trace: q6asm_audio_client_release() only calls kfree(ac) without freeing ac->port[n].buf, which is separately kzalloc'd in q6asm_map_memory_regions() (L516/L523). When q6asm_unmap_memory_regions() hits a DSP error (L407-410 goto err), it skips q6asm_audio_client_free_buf(), leaving port->buf live; q6asm_dai_compr_free() (L683-685) then unconditionally calls q6asm_audio_client_free(), so the kref release fires with non-NULL port->buf and leaks the allocation.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A local user with /dev/snd/ access opens a compressed audio stream, triggers q6asm_map_memory_regions(), then closes the device while inducing a DSP unmap failure (timeout or firmware error at __q6asm_memory_unmap). Each open/map/close cycle leaks a kzalloc'd audio_buffer array; repeated cycles exhaust kernel memory, causing system denial of service.
